\section{Repository Reproducibility Manifest}
\label{app:repro}

This appendix is repository-facing archival material rather than part of the main scientific reading path. It records a minimal reproduction recipe: data locations, artifact outputs, and a pinned repository state.
The machine-readable manifest \path{repro_manifest.json} stores the exact command blocks; this PDF appendix lists the corresponding artifacts and entry points.
Run \path{PYTHONPATH=. conda run -n fus-detectors python scripts/generate_repro_manifest.py} to refresh this manifest.

\paragraph{Repository state.}
Public repository: \path{git@github.com:ArthurShune/fus-detectors.git}.
Release tag: (not recorded; set \texttt{FUS\_DETECTORS\_RELEASE\_TAG} or create a git tag).
Commit: \texttt{53d827c} (full: \texttt{53d827ccb6}\allowbreak\texttt{bb6f0f1998}\allowbreak\texttt{d0d84c88c1}\allowbreak\texttt{79090a0730}); branch: \texttt{main}; dirty: \texttt{true}.
Dirty details: non-ignored paths=5, ignored build products=2.
Note: dirty status ignores common LaTeX build products (e.g., \texttt{*.aux, *.log, *.out, *.fls, *.fdb\_latexmk, *.synctex.gz, *.pdf}).
For archival reproducibility, generate this manifest from a clean, tagged release commit (\texttt{dirty=false}) and record the public repository URL and tag.

Conda spec: \path{environment.yml} (sha256: \texttt{653023c31d34f3ca}\allowbreak\texttt{8cf0e4f89b7c6aa1}\allowbreak\texttt{f1d3d1192604b391}\allowbreak\texttt{bdd0941d7ad53f79}).

Runtime (conda env \texttt{fus-detectors}):
Python: \path{3.11.9 (main, Apr 19 2024, 16:48:06) [GCC 11.2.0]}.
Platform: \path{Linux-6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2-x86_64-with-glibc2.39}.
Packages: \texttt{numpy}={1.26.4}, \texttt{scipy}={1.17.1}, \texttt{torch}={2.4.1}, \texttt{matplotlib}={3.10.0}, \texttt{cupy}={14.0.1}.
Torch CUDA: available=\texttt{true}, cuda=\texttt{12.1}, device0=\path{NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 SUPER}.

\paragraph{Datasets.}
Paths below are the expected on-disk locations used by the scripts in this repository. Large outputs under \path{runs/} and \path{reports/} are not tracked.
\begin{itemize}[nosep]
  \item \textbf{Brain-* k-Wave pilots (simulated IQ)}: Labeled low-FPR ROC + ablations.
    \\ Local: \path{runs/pilot/}.
    \\ Source: generated locally via sim/kwave/pilot\_motion.py (Makefile targets r4c-*).
  \item \textbf{Mace/Urban whole-brain mouse fUS (PD-only)}: PD-only telemetry + hold-out alias-gate evaluation (no Doppler IQ).
    \\ Local: \path{data/whole-brain-fUS}.
    \\ Source: \path{https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4905862}.
  \item \textbf{Shin RatBrain Fig3 (LOCA-ULM) beamformed IQ}: Real-IQ ingestion, telemetry, contract + label-free robustness sweeps.
    \\ Local archive: \path{data/shin_zenodo_10711806/RatBrain_Fig3.zip}.
    \\ Extracts to: \path{data/shin_zenodo_10711806/ratbrain_fig3_raw}.
    \\ Source: \path{https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.10711806}.
  \item \textbf{Twinkling artifact dataset (RawBCF phantoms)}: Structurally labeled phantom ROC (Gammex) + within-ensemble motion ladders + shrink-only regularizer hygiene (calculi).
    \\ Local: \path{data/twinkling_artifact/}.
    \\ Source: \path{https://doi.org/10.17816/DD76511}.
  \item \textbf{ULM Zenodo 7883227 (rat brain kHz IQ)}: Motion robustness, same-residual score audit, one-time baseline calibration sweep, and the single paper-facing same-acquisition structural audit using a published PALA localization-derived vascular reference.
    \\ Local archive: \path{data/ulm_zenodo_7883227/IQ_*.zip}.
    \\ Extracts to: \path{tmp/ulm_zenodo_7883227/}.
    \\ Source: \path{https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7883227}.
\end{itemize}

\paragraph{Pre-registered selections used in this manuscript.}
\begin{itemize}[nosep]
  \item \textbf{Brain low-FPR matrix (fixed-profile pilots)}: OpenSkull seed1; AliasContract seed2; SkullOR seed2; 64-frame windows at offsets {0,64,128,192,256}.
  \item \textbf{Shin all-clips telemetry}: IQData001-IQData080 (n=80), window 0:128.
  \item \textbf{Shin motion subset}: IQData001-005, IQData010, IQData020, IQData040, IQData060, IQData080 (n=10).
  \item \textbf{Mace holdout split (deduplicated)}: train: scan1/2 + scan3/6; test: scan4 + scan5.
  \item \textbf{Gammex flow phantom structural ROC (along-linear17)}: frames 0:85 (n=85), PRF=2500 Hz, N=17 shots.
  \item \textbf{Gammex flow phantom structural ROC (across-linear17)}: frames 0:200 (n=200), PRF=2500 Hz, N=17 shots.
  \item \textbf{Gammex flow phantom within-ensemble motion ladder}: along-linear17, frames 0:85, amps 0..2 px, kinds {rw,step}.
  \item \textbf{Twinkling calculi shrink-only regularizer hygiene}: calcifications sequence, frames 0:50 (n=50), PRF approx 500 Hz, N=9 shots.
  \item \textbf{ULM 7883227 baseline sweep}: blocks 1-3, frames 0:128, MC-SVD energy-frac sweep (label-free).
  \item \textbf{ULM 7883227 motion sweeps}: blocks 1-3, frames 0:128, frozen baseline e=0.975, motion kinds {brainlike,elastic}.
  \item \textbf{ULM 7883227 localization-derived structural audit (published PALA reference)}: reference/eval blocks 1-10, 64-frame windows at offsets {0,128}, max 2 windows/block, published PALA localization-derived vessel-core versus perivascular-shell reference, no-registration fully whitened matched-subspace variant. This supersedes the earlier 128-frame local-density surrogate and is the only paper-facing ULM structural endpoint.
\end{itemize}

\noindent\textit{ULM structural endpoint note.} The only paper-facing ULM structural benchmark in this appendix is the 64-frame same-acquisition structural audit that uses the published PALA localization-derived vascular reference and the no-registration fully whitened matched-subspace variant. Earlier 128-frame local-density surrogate runs were exploratory, are superseded, and do not support any manuscript claim.

    \end{itemize}

  \item \textbf{ULM 7883227 localization-derived structural audit (published PALA reference)}
    Paper refs:
    \begin{itemize}[nosep,leftmargin=0em,label={}]
      \item Front matter: ULM structural-audit hero figure (ulm7883227\_pala\_headline\_hero.pdf)
      \item ULM section: localization-derived structural audit on the same dataset
      \item ULM section: Table (ulm7883227\_pala\_structural\_roc\_table.tex)
      \item Supplement: Figure (ulm7883227\_pala\_structural\_roc\_curves.pdf)
      \item Discussion: RTX 4080 latency note for the same 64-frame no-registration fully whitened variant
    \end{itemize}
    Outputs:
    \begingroup\raggedright
    \begin{itemize}[nosep,leftmargin=0em,label={}]
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_structural_roc.csv}
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_structural_roc.json}
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_structural_roc_table.tex}
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_structural_roc_short32.json}
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_structural_roc_brainlike_}\allowbreak\path{motion.json}
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_latency_rtx4080.json}
      \item \path{figs/paper/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_headline_hero.pdf}
      \item \path{figs/paper/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_headline_hero.png}
      \item \path{figs/paper/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_structural_masks.pdf}
      \item \path{figs/paper/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_structural_roc_curves.pdf}
      \item \path{figs/paper/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_structural_roc_curves.png}
    \end{itemize}
    \endgroup
    Notes: This is the only paper-facing ULM structural endpoint: a bounded same-acquisition structural audit on real in vivo IQ using the published PALA localization rendering registered back onto the Zenodo IQ grid. The vessel-core and perivascular-shell masks are clipped to anatomical support before evaluation. It supersedes the earlier 128-frame local-density surrogate audit, which should be ignored for manuscript claims. It is stronger than a label-free audit but still not an independent multimodal task benchmark. The short-window detector itself runs without additional frame-to-frame registration in the frozen headline configuration because re-enabling registration changed the structural ROC only at the fourth decimal place while pushing latency well above the 64-frame acquisition budget.
    Exact command block: \path{repro_manifest.json}.
  \item \textbf{Fixed-calibration threshold-transfer audits (SIMUS/PyMUST + PALA-backed ULM)}
    Paper refs:
    \begin{itemize}[nosep,leftmargin=0em,label={}]
      \item Thresholding/evaluation protocol section: fixed-calibration transfer rationale
      \item SIMUS results: Table (simus\_fixed\_calibration\_transfer\_table.tex)
      \item ULM section: Table (ulm7883227\_pala\_fixed\_calibration\_transfer\_table.tex)
      \item Discussion: deployment-thresholding paragraph
    \end{itemize}
    Outputs:
    \begingroup\raggedright
    \begin{itemize}[nosep,leftmargin=0em,label={}]
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{fixed_calibration_transfer.json}
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{simus_v2/simus_fixed_calibration_}\allowbreak\path{transfer.csv}
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{simus_fixed_calibration_transfer_table.tex}
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_fixed_calibration_}\allowbreak\path{transfer.csv}
      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{ulm7883227_pala_fixed_calibration_transfer_}\allowbreak\path{table.tex}
    \end{itemize}
    \endgroup
    Notes: This is the paper's non-retrospective threshold-transfer check. On SIMUS/PyMUST, thresholds are learned once from separate calibration seeds 125--126 and applied unchanged to held-out seeds 127--128. On the bounded PALA-backed ULM endpoint, complementary 5-block calibration banks are used so that thresholds are fixed before evaluation on the held-out blocks. These are deployment-relevant transfer checks, not per-window oracle calibrations.
    Exact command block: \path{repro_manifest.json}.
  \item \textbf{SIMUS/PyMUST moving-scatterer sanity link + bundle contract (micro + alias; non-performance claims)}
    Paper refs:
    \begin{itemize}[nosep,leftmargin=0em,label={}]
      \item Simulation spec: moving-scatterer physical Doppler regime (SIMUS)
      \item Appendix (QC): physical-doppler sanity-link telemetry

    \begingroup\raggedright

      \item \path{reports/}\allowbreak\path{simus_contract/simus_pymust_paper_alias_}\allowbreak\path{seed0_hab_contract.txt}
      \item \path{runs/sim/simus_pymust_paper_micro_}\allowbreak\path{seed0/bundle/simus_pymust_paper_micro_seed0/}
      \item \path{runs/sim/simus_pymust_paper_alias_}\allowbreak\path{seed0/bundle/simus_pymust_paper_alias_seed0/}
      \item \path{runs/sim_eval/simus_baseline_compare_r1/}
    \end{itemize}
    \endgroup
    Notes: SIMUS/PyMUST runs are used as a moving-scatterer credibility anchor. Sanity-link summaries compare PSD-band / coherence / low-rank proxies against open real IQ; contract checks verify bundle integrity and regularizer-friendly regime telemetry. No performance claims are made from these comparisons.
    Exact command block: \path{repro_manifest.json}.
  \item \textbf{Paper build modes (paper vs supplement vs full)}
    (paper refs: Build packaging (Phase 7)).
    Outputs:
    \begingroup\raggedright
    \begin{itemize}[nosep,leftmargin=0em,label={}]
      \item \path{paper/preprint.pdf}
      \item \path{paper/supplement.pdf}
      \item \path{paper/methods_companion.pdf}
    \end{itemize}
    \endgroup
    Exact command block: \path{repro_manifest.json}.
\end{enumerate}
